Title: The maize brown midrib4 (bm4) gene encodes a functional folylpolyglutamate synthase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FPGS; Mur_Ligase_M domain-containing folate polyglutamylation enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; bm4-ref backcrossed into B73; Mu-induced bm4 alleles in B73-derived backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stalks; leaf midrib vascular and epidermal tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides secondary cell wall rigidity, limits solute transport across secondary cell walls, contributes to stalk strength; high lignin negatively affects ruminant digestion and biofuel production
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: one-carbon (C1) folate metabolism linked to lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: folate polyglutamylation / methyl-donor supply module upstream of methionine and SAM formation for lignin methylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glutamate residues to folate cofactors, generating polyglutamylated folates that serve as preferred substrates in C1 metabolism and support downstream 5-methyl-THF, methionine, and SAM production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in a wide variety of tissues in young seedlings and, to a lesser degree, mature plants; assayed in leaf midribs/stalk-related tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed broadly across many young tissues and some mature tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants; developmental stage affected lignin measurements (post-anthesis and post-senescence)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fine mapping; RNA-seq differential expression within candidate interval; qRT-PCR; Mu insertion allele analysis by Seq-Walking and PCR/Sanger sequencing; yeast met7 complementation; lignin quantification by Klason lignin and thioacidolysis; phloroglucinol-HCl staining
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (unreplicated candidate-gene expression screen); Seq-Walking transposon-flanking sequencing; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p413-ADH promoter in yeast complementation (constitutive, heterologous)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping, reduced transcript abundance, and multiple independent Mu insertions established GRMZM2G393334 as bm4. 2. bm4 encodes a functional folylpolyglutamate synthase, confirmed by complementation of the yeast met7 mutant. 3. bm4 mutants accumulate approximately 10–14% less lignin and show an increased S:G lignin ratio relative to wild type. 4. BM4 links folate-dependent C1 metabolism to lignin biosynthesis by supporting methyl-donor generation upstream of methionine/SAM-dependent lignin methylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal lignin accumulation and influences lignin monomer composition; loss of function reduces lignin content and increases S:G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to moderately reduce lignin for improved forage digestibility or biofuel processing; should be deployed cautiously because it acts in central folate/C1 metabolism and may be better used as partial-loss or allele-specific engineering rather than complete disruption
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bm4 mutants show brown midrib phenotype and modest lignin reduction; severe growth defects were reported in bm2-bm4 double mutants; central metabolic role suggests risk of developmental defects if FPGS activity is too strongly reduced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The effect of bm4 on folate accumulation was not determined. bm4 likely acts upstream of bm2/MTHFR, and the reference allele appears leaky. The causal lesion in bm4-ref was not definitively resolved, although coding-region deletions and reduced transcript accumulation were observed.
